CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Use Platform-Native Security Controls
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Runtime Security**. Verifies that AI agents use platform-native security controls such as Kubernetes Pod Security Standards (Restricted profile), GKE Binary Authorization, AWS ECS task security profiles, or equivalent mechanisms. These controls enforce a hardened baseline at the platform level, independent of application-level settings, reducing the risk of misconfiguration.
Risk: AI agents deployed without platform-native security controls rely entirely on application-level configurations that are easily misconfigured or bypassed. Platform controls such as Pod Security Standards, Binary Authorization, and task-level security policies provide an independent enforcement layer that limits the impact of misconfigurations in individual agent manifests.

Code.Other:
1. For Kubernetes: enable Pod Security Standards at the Restricted level on all namespaces running agent workloads (kubectl label namespace <ns> pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted).
2. For GKE: enable Binary Authorization to require signed image attestations before deployment.
3. For AWS ECS: apply task security profiles and enable container-level seccomp via ECS task definition.
4. For Azure AKS: use Azure Policy for Kubernetes to enforce Pod Security Standards.
5. Regularly audit policy exceptions and exceptions must be time-limited and approved.

Recommendation.Text: Enable platform-native security controls (Kubernetes Pod Security Standards Restricted profile, Binary Authorization, or equivalent) on all namespaces and clusters running agent workloads. Audit and minimise policy exceptions.

Notes: ASPM-067 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
